prose: Fukui reactivity indices were computed for ferrocene (21 atoms: Fe, 10 C, 10 H) using the GFN2-xTB semi-empirical tight-binding method (xtb backend), gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. Geometry was taken directly from the user-supplied ferrocene.xyz without re-optimization. The finite-difference Fukui scheme uses Mulliken population analysis on the neutral (N), cation (N−1, charge +1, mult 2), and anion (N+1, charge −1, mult 2) states. The headline value reported is f⁻(Fe) = 0.25938191 (most nucleophilic site). The iron atom (atom 11) dominates both f⁺ = 0.22885456 (most electrophilic) and f⁻ = 0.25938191 (most nucleophilic), consistent with Fe 3d HOMO character. The dual descriptor on Fe is −0.030527354, indicating preferential nucleophilic character at iron — in agreement with ferrocene's known electron-donor reactivity. Cp carbon and hydrogen atoms carry small, positive Fukui values (~0.014 and ~0.063 respectively), reflecting their subordinate role. Integrity checks: sum(f⁺) = 1.0000, sum(f⁻) = 1.0000; status: ok, trustworthy: true. No engine warnings were raised. CLI: /home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py fukui --method xtb --charge 0 --mult 1 /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/ferrocene/ferrocene.xyz
